title: Communication Bottlenecks
concept: Splitting models requires GPUs to constantly exchange data; high-speed interconnects (like NVLink) are essential to prevent communication from stalling math.

exercises.prompt: When a large AI model is split across multiple GPUs, how do the GPUs work together?

options.text: They never need to talk to each other.
They must constantly exchange data to coordinate math.
They only talk once at the very end of training.

exercises.explain: When models are too big for one chip, we split them across multiple GPUs. However, these chips must constantly talk to each other to finish the math.

cards.title: The Speed Trap
cards.body: Did you know that splitting a massive AI across multiple superfast chips can actually make it run *slower*? If the connection between those chips is sluggish, the chips spend all their time waiting to talk instead of thinking!

cards.title: What is a Communication Bottleneck?
cards.body: A communication bottleneck occurs when powerful GPUs waste time waiting for data to travel between them. Because split models require constant teamwork, they need specialized high-speed interconnects (like NVIDIA's NVLink) to trade data instantly and keep their math engines running.

cards.title: The Mailroom Delay
cards.body: Imagine a group project where four friends are writing a massive book together. Instead of working in the same room, they are forced to mail their pages to each other using slow postal mail. Even if each person writes incredibly fast, they spend most of their time sitting around waiting for the mail carrier to arrive. In AI, slow wires act like the postal mail, forcing fast GPUs to sit idle.

cards.title: Waiting on the Web
cards.body: Imagine training a massive AI across two standard computers connected by basic office internet. The graphics cards can perform billions of math operations per second, but they end up running at less than 5% capacity because they are constantly waiting for the network cable to transmit the model's next layer.

cards.title: Myth: Faster GPUs Always Win
cards.body: Many people think that getting faster GPUs will always make AI training faster. But if your communication cables are slow, upgrading your chips won't help! The faster chips will simply wait faster. The connection speed is what actually unlocks their power.

cards.title: Slow Lanes vs. Expressways
cards.body: Compare how data flows between GPUs under different connection setups. Without specialized links, the system gets clogged.

left.text: GPUs spend most of their time idle, waiting for data to travel over slow, traditional cables.

right.text: Data flies between GPUs instantly, keeping the math processors running at 100% speed.

exercises.prompt: What role do high-speed interconnects (like NVLink) play in split-model training?

options.text: They make the math calculations easier to solve.
They allow GPUs to share data rapidly so they don't sit idle.
They reduce the electricity bill of the server room.
They store the entire model so the GPUs don't have to.

exercises.explain: High-speed interconnects like NVLink act as super-fast highways, allowing GPUs to trade data rapidly so they don't stall their calculations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you split an AI model across multiple GPUs, they must constantly exchange data during training.
exercises.explain: Splitting a model requires continuous communication between the chips to share active math results. Without fast connections, the chips waste time waiting.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of high-speed GPU connections.
exercises.sentence: To prevent slow communication from stalling math, AI setups use specialized high-speed interconnects to link GPUs together.
exercises.explain: To prevent communication bottlenecks, AI clusters use specialized high-speed interconnects (like NVLink) to move data between GPUs almost instantly.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios describes a communication bottleneck?

options.text: An AI model gets a memory error because it is too large to load on a single GPU.
A GPU pauses its calculations for several milliseconds because it is waiting for layer data from another GPU.
A GPU processes a matrix multiplication in record time using its tensor cores.

exercises.explain: Option B describes a communication bottleneck where the GPU is idle ("waiting") because data transfer is slow. Option A is a memory issue, and Option C is standard computation.

exercises.prompt: Why does a slow communication network between GPUs slow down the training of a split AI model?

options.text: The GPUs run out of memory because the model is too small to fit.
The GPUs overheat and shut down due to the intense calculations.
The GPUs waste valuable time waiting for data to travel over slow wires.
The model becomes too smart and refuses to process any more math.

exercises.explain: When model parts are split across multiple GPUs, they must send and receive intermediate data constantly. If the communication wires are slow, the superfast processing units spend most of their time waiting instead of calculating. High-speed links like NVLink solve this bottleneck.
